steps.args: {'prompt': 'From this Netflix 10-K for fiscal year 2019, extract: (1) total streaming revenues for 2019 and 2018, (2) global average paying memberships for 2019 and 2018, (3) average monthly revenue per paying membership for 2019 and 2018 (both global and by region if available), (4) paid memberships at end of period. Quote the exact figures and units. {{nflx_10k_2019}}'}
steps.result:
# Netflix FY2019 10-K — Extracted Figures

All figures below are quoted exactly as presented in the filing (Item 7, MD&A, "Results of Operations" and regional tables; amounts in thousands unless otherwise noted).

## 1. Total Streaming Revenues
| Metric | 2019 | 2018 |
|---|---|---|
| Streaming revenues | **$19,859,230** (thousands, i.e., ~$19.86 billion) | **$15,428,752** (thousands, i.e., ~$15.43 billion) |

*Note: Total revenues (including DVD) were $20,156,447 thousand (2019) and $15,794,341 thousand (2018); DVD revenues were $297,217 and $365,589 thousand, respectively.*

## 2. Global Average Paying Memberships
| Metric | 2019 | 2018 |
|---|---|---|
| Average paying memberships | **152,984** (thousands, i.e., ~153.0 million) | **124,658** (thousands, i.e., ~124.7 million) |

## 3. Average Monthly Revenue per Paying Membership
**Global:**
| 2019 | 2018 |
|---|---|
| **$10.82** | **$10.31** (a 5% increase) |

**By region (streaming):**
| Region | 2019 | 2018 | Reported change |
|---|---|---|---|
| United States and Canada (UCAN) | **$12.57** | **$11.16** | +13% (+13% constant currency) |
| Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) | **$10.33** | **$10.45** | −1% (+4% constant currency) |
| Latin America (LATAM) | **$8.21** | **$8.19** | ~0% (+13% constant currency) |
| Asia-Pacific (APAC) | **$9.24** | **$9.33** | −1% (+3% constant currency) |

## 4. Paid Memberships at End of Period
| Metric | 2019 | 2018 |
|---|---|---|
| Global streaming paid memberships at end of period | **167,090** (thousands, i.e., ~167.1 million) | **139,259** (thousands, i.e., ~139.3 million) |

**By region, end of 2019 (thousands):** UCAN 67,662; EMEA 51,778; LATAM 31,417; APAC 16,233. Paid net additions in 2019 were 27,831 thousand globally.

*Definition per the filing: "A paid membership (also referred to as a paid subscription) is defined as a membership that has the right to receive Netflix service following sign-up and a method of payment being provided, and that is not part of a free trial or other promotional offering."*

steps.args: {'prompt': 'From this Netflix 10-K for fiscal year 2024, extract: (1) total streaming revenues for 2024 and 2023, (2) global average paying memberships for 2024 and 2023, (3) average monthly revenue per paying membership for 2024 and 2023 (both global and by region if available), (4) paid memberships at end of period. Quote the exact figures and units. {{nflx_10k_2024}}'}
steps.result:
# Netflix FY2024 10-K — Requested Figures

All figures below are quoted from the "Results of Operations" section (Part II, Item 7) and Note 2 (Revenue Recognition). Table units are "in thousands, except revenue per membership and percentages."

## 1. Total Streaming Revenues

| | FY2024 | FY2023 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Streaming revenues | **$39,000,966** (thousand) = ~$39.0 billion | **$33,640,458** (thousand) = ~$33.6 billion | +16% |

Note: Total revenues were $39,000,966 thousand (2024) vs. $33,723,297 thousand (2023); 2023 included $82,839 thousand of DVD revenues (DVD-by-mail service was discontinued in 2023). Hedging gains of $124 million are included in 2024 streaming revenues; none in 2023.

## 2. Global Average Paying Memberships

| | FY2024 | FY2023 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Average paying memberships | **277,730** (thousand) = ~277.7 million | **240,889** (thousand) = ~240.9 million | +15% |

## 3. Average Monthly Revenue per Paying Membership (ARM)

**Global:**
| | FY2024 | FY2023 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| ARM | **$11.70** | **$11.64** | +1% (constant currency: +4%) |

**By region:**

| Region | FY2024 | FY2023 | Change | Constant Currency Change |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| UCAN (US & Canada) | **$17.20** | **$16.28** | +6% ($0.92) | +6% |
| EMEA | **$10.96** | **$10.87** | +1% ($0.09) | +1% |
| LATAM | **$8.24** | **$8.66** | −5% ($(0.42)) | +21% |
| APAC | **$7.29** | **$7.64** | −5% ($(0.35)) | −3% |

The LATAM divergence (−5% reported vs. +21% constant currency) reflects "the devaluation of the Argentine peso relative to the U.S. dollar coupled with significant price increases in the local currency."

## 4. Paid Memberships at End of Period

| | FY2024 | FY2023 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Global paid memberships** | **301,626** (thousand) = ~301.6 million | **260,276** (thousand) = ~260.3 million | +16% |
| Paid net membership additions | 41,350 (thousand) | 29,529 (thousand) | +40% |

**By region (end of period, in thousands):**

| Region | FY2024 | FY2023 |
|---|---|---|
| UCAN | 89,625 | 80,128 |
| EMEA | 101,133 | 88,813 |
| LATAM | 53,327 | 45,997 |
| APAC | 57,541 | 45,338 |

**Definition note (from filing):** A paid membership is one that "has the right to receive Netflix service following sign-up and a method of payment being provided, and that is not part of a free trial or certain other promotions." Extra member sub accounts are **not** included in paid memberships. The filing's Business section rounds this to "approximately 302 million paid memberships in over 190 countries."

steps.args:
{'final_result': 'Netflix's Average Revenue Per Paying User — which Netflix reports as "average monthly revenue per paying membership" (ARM) — increased modestly from 2019 to 2024, rising from $10.82/month to $11.70/month, an increase of $0.88, or +8.13% over the five-year period (a CAGR of only ~1.58% per year).

**Global figures (from Netflix's 10-K filings):**

| Metric | FY2019 | FY2024 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Streaming revenues | $19,859,230K (~$19.86B) | $39,000,966K (~$39.00B) | +96.39% |
| Average paying memberships | 152,984K (~153.0M) | 277,730K (~277.7M) | +81.54% |
| Avg. monthly revenue per paying membership (ARM) | **$10.82** | **$11.70** | **+$0.88 (+8.13%)** |
| Implied annual revenue per paying membership | ~$129.81 | ~$140.43 | +8.18% |

**Verification:** 
- 2019: $19,859,230K ÷ 152,984K memberships ÷ 12 months ≈ $10.82/month ✓
- 2024: $39,000,966K ÷ 277,730K memberships ÷ 12 months ≈ $11.70/month ✓
- % change: ($11.70 − $10.82) / $10.82 = 8.13%; CAGR = (11.70/10.82)^(1/5) − 1 ≈ 1.58%/year

**Regional breakdown (monthly ARM, 2019 → 2024):**
- US & Canada (UCAN): $12.57 → $17.20 (+36.83%) — driven by repeated price increases
- EMEA: $10.33 → $10.96 (+6.10%)
- Latin America (LATAM): $8.21 → $8.24 (+0.37%) — strong local-currency price increases largely offset by currency devaluation (notably the Argentine peso)
- Asia-Pacific (APAC): $9.24 → $7.29 (−21.10%) — reflecting lower-priced plans (e.g., mobile plans) and price cuts in some markets to drive membership growth, plus currency headwinds

**Interpretation:** Over 2019–2024, Netflix's revenue growth (+96%) was driven overwhelmingly by membership growth (+82% in average paying memberships, boosted by the paid-sharing crackdown and the ad-supported tier) rather than by per-user monetization, which grew only ~8% globally. The flat-to-modest global ARM masks divergent regional trends: strong monetization gains in North America (+37%) were largely offset by declining ARM in APAC (−21%) and flat LATAM figures, as Netflix's international expansion mix-shifted toward lower-priced markets and plans, and foreign currency depreciation weighed on U.S. dollar-reported ARM.

{
    "sources": [
        {
            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528020000040/form10kq419.htm",
            "name": "Netflix, Inc. Form 10-K for fiscal year 2019 (filed January 29, 2020)"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm",
            "name": "Netflix, Inc. Form 10-K for fiscal year 2024 (filed January 27, 2025)"
        }
    ]
}'}
steps.result:
Netflix's Average Revenue Per Paying User — which Netflix reports as "average monthly revenue per paying membership" (ARM) — increased modestly from 2019 to 2024, rising from $10.82/month to $11.70/month, an increase of $0.88, or +8.13% over the five-year period (a CAGR of only ~1.58% per year).

**Global figures (from Netflix's 10-K filings):**

| Metric | FY2019 | FY2024 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Streaming revenues | $19,859,230K (~$19.86B) | $39,000,966K (~$39.00B) | +96.39% |
| Average paying memberships | 152,984K (~153.0M) | 277,730K (~277.7M) | +81.54% |
| Avg. monthly revenue per paying membership (ARM) | **$10.82** | **$11.70** | **+$0.88 (+8.13%)** |
| Implied annual revenue per paying membership | ~$129.81 | ~$140.43 | +8.18% |

**Verification:** 
- 2019: $19,859,230K ÷ 152,984K memberships ÷ 12 months ≈ $10.82/month ✓
- 2024: $39,000,966K ÷ 277,730K memberships ÷ 12 months ≈ $11.70/month ✓
- % change: ($11.70 − $10.82) / $10.82 = 8.13%; CAGR = (11.70/10.82)^(1/5) − 1 ≈ 1.58%/year

**Regional breakdown (monthly ARM, 2019 → 2024):**
- US & Canada (UCAN): $12.57 → $17.20 (+36.83%) — driven by repeated price increases
- EMEA: $10.33 → $10.96 (+6.10%)
- Latin America (LATAM): $8.21 → $8.24 (+0.37%) — strong local-currency price increases largely offset by currency devaluation (notably the Argentine peso)
- Asia-Pacific (APAC): $9.24 → $7.29 (−21.10%) — reflecting lower-priced plans (e.g., mobile plans) and price cuts in some markets to drive membership growth, plus currency headwinds

**Interpretation:** Over 2019–2024, Netflix's revenue growth (+96%) was driven overwhelmingly by membership growth (+82% in average paying memberships, boosted by the paid-sharing crackdown and the ad-supported tier) rather than by per-user monetization, which grew only ~8% globally. The flat-to-modest global ARM masks divergent regional trends: strong monetization gains in North America (+37%) were largely offset by declining ARM in APAC (−21%) and flat LATAM figures, as Netflix's international expansion mix-shifted toward lower-priced markets and plans, and foreign currency depreciation weighed on U.S. dollar-reported ARM.
